positions.detail: Down -99% — stop loss triggered at -50%. Close position.

agent_analysis:
## POSITION MONITOR — Thursday (ELEVATED)

### HOOD CALL: ☠️ TOTAL LOSS

| Metric | Value | Threshold |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Stock move | **-12.8%** | Catastrophic against call thesis |
| Option P&L | **-99%** | Hard stop (-50%) blown through |
| Status | STOP_HIT | Should have exited at -50% |
| Estimated delta | **~0.01** | Delta stop (< 0.10) also triggered |

Stock dropped 12.8% against a call — this option is functionally worthless. With 1 day to expiry and delta near zero, there is no recovery scenario. The -50% hard stop was triggered and the position should have been closed at that point; the additional -49% loss beyond stop represents slippage or delayed execution.

**Post-mortem flag:** This is a -99% loss on a position where the stop was -50%. The reflection engine needs to investigate whether the stop was not executable (gap down, illiquidity) or was simply not enforced. Either way, this is the kind of outcome that the 3% Kelly cap exists to contain — but it still represents a max-severity loss.

---

### Portfolio Status

| Position | P&L | Action |
|----------|-----|--------|
| HOOD CALL | -99% | **CLOSE NOW** — market order, accept any residual bid |

Only 1 of 3 positions visible. If the other two positions exist, they need Thursday ELEVATED review immediately — theta is accelerating into tomorrow's close.

---

## RECOMMENDATION

**CLOSE HOOD immediately via market order.** The option is near-zero but sell for whatever penny bid exists rather than let it expire worthless — recoup any residual value. Flag for reflection: a 12.8% adverse stock move on a weekly call is a regime event. Review whether direction signals (momentum model, flow conviction) gave false confidence, and whether this ticker's weekly ATR% justified the position size. **No new action possible — damage is done, capture lessons.**
